mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediately through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as support rather than panic response.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25pp over the latest 2 monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are negative or below baseline in triggered easing episodes, or if the effect disappears when CPIAUCSL is rising on a 3-month annualized basis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE is a direct recession-risk signal: it implies weaker household income growth, lower consumption, and higher equity risk premia, so SPX returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days after a clear unemployment deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if PAYEMS growth offsets the signal and the combined effect becomes indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which supports earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should be visible only when sales growth is broad enough to matter for aggregate demand, not when it is a one-month noise spike.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS is higher than its 6-month average for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if above-average RSAFS periods do not outperform baseline SPX returns over 60 trading days, or if the signal fails after controlling for contemporaneous GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in INDPRO combined with stronger HOUST signals a synchronized supply-side improvement: firms are producing more and residential construction is adding activity, which supports cyclical earnings and positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint INDPRO-HOUST improvement does not outperform each series alone in predicting positive 20d SPX returns, or if the combined signal is negative in more than half of matched episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A GDP downshift in GDPC1 is a broad demand-slowdown warning that should weaken risk appetite and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect is clearest when the slowdown persists across at least two quarterly observations rather than a single revision-driven dip.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior-quarter growth rate for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive GDP decelerations fail to predict lower 60d SPX returns versus baseline, or if the sign turns positive in most post-deceleration windows.

mechanism_narrative: A high policy rate with sticky inflation compresses valuation multiples and raises real discount rates, so when FEDFUNDS remains elevated while CPIAUCSL stays firm, SPX returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days. This is a short-horizon repricing effect rather than a slow growth effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation is positive
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate, sticky-inflation episodes are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Fast payroll growth in PAYEMS indicates resilient labor income and spending capacity, which should support equity prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when payroll gains are entirely offset by rising UNRATE, because that combination implies labor-market churn rather than healthy expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month payroll gains do not predict higher 20d SPX returns, or if episodes with simultaneous PAYEMS gains and rising UNRATE produce negative returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: A rising INDPRO without an accompanying surge in HOUST signals broad industrial strength with manageable overheating, which should support cyclically sensitive earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. If housing construction accelerates too sharply, the mechanism breaks because it signals tightening capacity and rates pressure rather than clean growth.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is above its 6-month average and HOUST is not rising more than 5% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-led expansion does not predict positive 60d SPX returns after excluding housing overheating periods, or if strong INDPRO with rising HOUST produces negative returns more often than positive ones.
